Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

Interface IAutoTacticRegistry

All Known Implementing Classes:
AutoTacticRegistry
public interface IAutoTacticRegistry
Common protocol for accessing the Auto Tactic Registry.

The Auto Tactic Registry manages tactic ids that are known to the Sequent Prover. A tactic id is registered only if it has been registered through the autoTactics extension point.

This registry is not used by the sequent prover itself, but is provided as a service to clients that want to register and retrieve auto tactics.

An auto tactic is a tactic that does not depend on user inputs. All auto tactics that are registered using this registry are stateless. They are constructed using the default constructor.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • SequentProver.getAutoTacticRegistry()

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ICombinatorDescriptor
    getCombinatorDescriptor(String id)
    Returns combinator descriptor with given id, or a placeholder if it is not registered.
    ICombinatorDescriptor[]
    getCombinatorDescriptors()
    Returns descriptors for all registered tactic combinators.
    IDynamicTacticRef
    getDynTacticRef(String id)
    Returns a reference to the dynamic tactic with the given id if it exists at call time, else a reference to a placeholder.
    IDynamicTacticRef[]
    getDynTacticRefs()
    Returns references to the dynamic tactics provided at call time.

    Returns parameterizer descriptor with given id, or a placeholder if it is not registered.

    Returns descriptors for all registered tactic parameterizers.

    Returns the ids of all tactic extensions that have been registered.

    Checks if a tactic extension with the given tactic id is present in the auto tactic registry.

    Returns whether the given id corresponds to a registered tactic combinator.
    boolean
    isRegisteredParameterizer(String id)
    Returns whether the given id corresponds to a registered tactic parameterizer.

  • Method Details

    • isRegistered

      boolean isRegistered(String tacticID)
      Checks if a tactic extension with the given tactic id is present in the auto tactic registry.

      This is fully equivalent to Arrays.asList(getTacticIDs()).contains(tacticID), although implemented in a more efficient way.

      Parameters:
      tacticID - the tactic id to check for
      Returns:
      true iff the given tactic id is known to the tactic registry

    • getRegisteredIDs

      String[] getRegisteredIDs()
      Returns the ids of all tactic extensions that have been registered.

      The order of the returned array is of no significance and may change across multiple calls of the method.

      Returns:
      an array of all registered tactic ids

    • getTacticDescriptor

      ITacticDescriptor getTacticDescriptor(String tacticID) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the tactic descriptor of the tactic extension with the given id.

      Clients should first use isRegistered(String) to check if a tactic extension with the given id is registered. In case no tactic extension with the given id has been registered a placeholder is returned. Place holder tactics always fail.

      Parameters:
      tacticID - the id of the tactic
      Returns:
      the tactic descriptor of the tactic with the given id
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException
      Since:
      3.0

    • isRegisteredParameterizer

      boolean isRegisteredParameterizer(String id)
      Returns whether the given id corresponds to a registered tactic parameterizer.
      Parameters:
      id - a parameterizer id
      Returns:
      true if registered, false otherwise
      Since:
      3.0

    • getParameterizerDescriptors

      IParameterizerDescriptor[] getParameterizerDescriptors()
      Returns descriptors for all registered tactic parameterizers.

      The order of the returned array is of no significance and may change across multiple calls of the method.

      Returns:
      an array of instantiators
      Since:
      2.3

    • getParameterizerDescriptor

      IParameterizerDescriptor getParameterizerDescriptor(String id)
      Returns parameterizer descriptor with given id, or a placeholder if it is not registered.
      Parameters:
      id - a parameterizer id
      Returns:
      a parameterizer descriptor
      Since:
      2.3

    • isRegisteredCombinator

      boolean isRegisteredCombinator(String id)
      Returns whether the given id corresponds to a registered tactic combinator.
      Parameters:
      id - a combinator id
      Returns:
      true if registered, false otherwise
      Since:
      3.0

    • getCombinatorDescriptors

      ICombinatorDescriptor[] getCombinatorDescriptors()
      Returns descriptors for all registered tactic combinators.

      The order of the returned array is of no significance and may change across multiple calls of the method.

      Returns:
      an array of combinator descriptors
      Since:
      2.3

    • getCombinatorDescriptor

      ICombinatorDescriptor getCombinatorDescriptor(String id)
      Returns combinator descriptor with given id, or a placeholder if it is not registered.
      Parameters:
      id - a combinator id
      Returns:
      a combinator descriptor
      Since:
      2.3

    • getDynTacticRefs

      IDynamicTacticRef[] getDynTacticRefs()
      Returns references to the dynamic tactics provided at call time.

      The returned references will construct the dynamic tactic instance when ITacticDescriptor.getTacticInstance() is called, by fetching the dynamic tactic with the given id at call time.

      Returns:
      the dynamic tactic references
      Since:
      3.0

    • getDynTacticRef

      IDynamicTacticRef getDynTacticRef(String id)
      Returns a reference to the dynamic tactic with the given id if it exists at call time, else a reference to a placeholder.

      Even if the returned reference contains a placeholder, calling ITacticDescriptor.getTacticInstance() later on will fetch in this registry the dynamic tactic that is available at that moment.

      Parameters:
      id - a dynamic tactic id
      Returns:
      a dynamic tactic reference
      Since:
      3.0
